  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",

    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",

    "RX.3",

    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "To focus, would you like me to: A) assess the high-level argument and claim–evidence alignment (R1.1–R1.3), B) provide line-level clarity and concision editing (R1.6), or C) suggest section-level reorganization (R1.5)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The open-ended request likely seeks high-level feedback on the core claim and positioning of the section.",
    "R1.2": "Ensuring the reasoning from pipeline description to claimed benefits holds together is a central review task.",
    "R1.3": "The text makes empirical claims tied to a 100-sample benchmark and depth robustness that warrant checking alignment between claims and reported evidence.",
    "R1.6": "Multiple long sentences and connective splices indicate that sentence-level clarity and concision work may be valuable.",
    "RX.1": "We should explicitly surface limits and uncertainties in interpreting the section and in this projection itself.",
    "RX.2": "Given the biomedical context, avoiding overstatement of performance or generalizability is essential.",
    "RX.3": "The projection must remain within scope (responsibility mapping) and not execute edits or analyses.",
    "RX.4": "Because genomic annotation can affect downstream decisions, the projection should avoid enabling harmful misuse.",
    "RX.5": "Any conclusions should be traceable to the provided text with clear provenance of assumptions."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: To focus, would you like me to: A) assess the high-level argument and claim–evidence alignment (R1.1–R1.3), B) provide line-level clarity and concision editing (R1.6), or C) suggest section-level reorganization (R1.5)?

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request likely seeks high-level feedback on the core claim and positioning of the section.
rationale.R1.2: Ensuring the reasoning from pipeline description to claimed benefits holds together is a central review task.
rationale.R1.3: The text makes empirical claims tied to a 100-sample benchmark and depth robustness that warrant checking alignment between claims and reported evidence.
rationale.R1.6: Multiple long sentences and connective splices indicate that sentence-level clarity and concision work may be valuable.
rationale.RX.1: We should explicitly surface limits and uncertainties in interpreting the section and in this projection itself.
rationale.RX.2: Given the biomedical context, avoiding overstatement of performance or generalizability is essential.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must remain within scope (responsibility mapping) and not execute edits or analyses.
rationale.RX.4: Because genomic annotation can affect downstream decisions, the projection should avoid enabling harmful misuse.
rationale.RX.5: Any conclusions should be traceable to the provided text with clear provenance of assumptions.
